title: Pole Test Protocol for Motor Function Assessment in Mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8tsfc","text":"This protocol was used to assess general motor skills and balance on grafted animals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bhs4n","text":"Ensure consistent environmental conditions (noise, light, temperature).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2sav4","text":"Use the same pole and orientation throughout the experiment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":8,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f6rm6","text":"Observe for abnormal behaviors or jumping, which may invalidate a trial.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Train animals for at least 2 consecutive days before the actual test. Each day, perform 3 mock trials per animal using the same procedure as the test. Ensure mice are acclimated and capable of descending the pole.
Place the mouse facing upwards (head toward the top) on top of the 20 cm vertical pole (preferably inside the home cage).
Begin timing once the mouse rotates and orients downward (i.e., head pointing toward the ground).

Allow the mouse to climb down naturally and stop time when mouse touches the floor. If the mouse does not descend within 1 minute , stop the trial and allow the animal to rest. For incomplete or timed-out trials, repeat after a minimum rest period of 10 minutes .
Perform a total of 3 successful trials per animal .
Calculate the mean descent time (in seconds) across the 3 valid trials for each animal.
creator: Roberto Garcia Swinburn
